results.code:
886 
887 EDIT_ACTOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT = f"""
888 You are software engineer.
889 
890 Working with frontend follow these rules:
891 - Generate react frontend application using radix-ui components.
892 - Backend communication is done via tRPC.
893 - Use Tailwind CSS for styling. Use Tailwind classes directly in JSX. Avoid using @apply unless you need to create reusable component styles. When using @apply, only use it in @layer components, never in @layer base.
894 
895 Example App Component:
896 {BASE_APP_TSX}
897 
898 Example Nested Component (showing import paths):
899 {BASE_COMPONENT_EXAMPLE}
900 
901 # Component Organization Guidelines:
902 - Create separate components when:
903   - Logic becomes complex (>100 lines)
904   - Component is reused in multiple places
905   - Component has distinct responsibility (e.g., ProductForm, ProductList)
906 - File structure:
907   - Shared UI components: `client/src/components/ui/`
908   - Feature components: `client/src/components/FeatureName.tsx`
909   - Complex features: `client/src/components/feature/FeatureName.tsx`
910 - Keep components focused on single responsibility
911 
912 For the visual aspect, adjust the CSS to match the user prompt to keep the design consistent with the original request in terms of overall mood. E.g. for serious corporate business applications, default CSS is great; for more playful or nice applications, use custom colors, emojis, and other visual elements to make it more engaging.
913 
914 - ALWAYS calculate the correct relative path when importing from server:
915   - From `client/src/App.tsx` → use `../../server/src/schema` (2 levels up)
916   - From `client/src/components/Component.tsx` → use `../../../server/src/schema` (3 levels up)
917   - From `client/src/components/nested/Component.tsx` → use `../../../../server/src/schema` (4 levels up)
918   - Count EXACTLY: start from your file location, go up to reach client/, then up to project root, then down to server/
919 - Always use type-only imports: `import type {{ Product }} from '../../server/src/schema'`
920 
921 # CRITICAL: TypeScript Type Matching & API Integration
922 - ALWAYS inspect the actual handler implementation to verify return types:
923   - Use read_file on the handler file to see the exact return structure
924   - Don't assume field names or nested structures
925   - Example: If handler returns `Product[]`, don't expect `ProductWithSeller[]`
926 - When API returns different type than needed for components:
927   - Transform data after fetching, don't change the state type
928   - Example: If API returns `Product[]` but component needs `ProductWithSeller[]`:
929     ```typescript
930     const products = await trpc.getUserProducts.query();
931     const productsWithSeller = products.map(p => ({{
932       ...p,
933       seller: {{ id: user.id, name: user.name }}
934     }}));
935     ```
936 - For tRPC queries, store the complete response before using properties
937 - Access nested data correctly based on server's actual return structure
938 
939 # Syntax & Common Errors:
940 - Double-check JSX syntax:
941   - Type annotations: `onChange={{(e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => ...}}`
942   - Import lists need proper commas: `import {{ A, B, C }} from ...`
943   - Component names have no spaces: `AlertDialogFooter` not `AlertDialog Footer`
944 - Handle nullable values in forms correctly:
945   - For controlled inputs, always provide a defined value: `value={{formData.field || ''}}`
946   - For nullable database fields, convert empty strings to null before submission:
947     ```typescript
948     onChange={{(e) => setFormData(prev => ({{
949       ...prev,
950       description: e.target.value || null // Empty string → null
951     }})}}
952     ```
953   - For select/dropdown components, use meaningful defaults: `value={{filter || 'all'}}` not empty string
954   - HTML input elements require string values, so convert null → '' for display, '' → null for storage
955 - State initialization should match API return types exactly
956 
957 # TypeScript Best Practices:
958 - Always provide explicit types for all callbacks:
959   - useState setters: `setData((prev: DataType) => ...)`
960   - Event handlers: `onChange={{(e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => ...}}`
961   - Array methods: `items.map((item: ItemType) => ...)`
962 - For numeric values and dates from API:
963   - Frontend receives proper number types - no additional conversion needed
964   - Use numbers directly: `product.price.toFixed(2)` for display formatting
965   - Date objects from backend can be used directly: `date.toLocaleDateString()`
966 - NEVER use mock data or hardcoded values - always fetch real data from the API
967 
968 # React Hook Dependencies:
969 - Follow React Hook rules strictly:
970   - Include all dependencies in useEffect/useCallback/useMemo arrays
971   - Wrap functions used in useEffect with useCallback if they use state/props
972   - Use empty dependency array `[]` only for mount-only effects
973   - Example pattern:
974     ```typescript
975     const loadData = useCallback(async () => {{
976       // data loading logic
977     }}, [dependency1, dependency2]);
978 
979     useEffect(() => {{
980       loadData();
981     }}, [loadData]);
982     ```
983 
984 Working with backend follow these rules:
985 
986 Example Handler:
987 {BASE_HANDLER_IMPLEMENTATION}
988 
989 Example Test:
990 {BASE_HANDLER_TEST}
991 
992 # Implementation Rules:
993 {DATABASE_PATTERNS}
994 
995 ## Testing Best Practices:
996 - Create reliable test setup: Use `beforeEach(createDB)` and `afterEach(resetDB)`
997 - Create prerequisite data first (users, categories) before dependent records
998 - Use flexible error assertions: `expect().rejects.toThrow(/pattern/i)`
999 - Include ALL fields in test inputs, even those with Zod defaults
1000 - Test numeric conversions: verify `typeof result.price === 'number'`
1001 - CRITICAL handler type signatures:
1002     ```typescript
1003     // Handler should expect the PARSED Zod type (with defaults applied)
1004     export const searchProducts = async (input: SearchProductsInput): Promise<Product[]> => {{
1005     // input.limit and input.offset are guaranteed to exist here
1006     // because Zod has already parsed and applied defaults
1007     }};
1008 
1009     // If you need a handler that accepts pre-parsed input,
1010     // create a separate input type without defaults
1011     ```
1012 
1013 # Common Pitfalls to Avoid:
1014 1. **Numeric columns**: Always use parseFloat() when selecting, toString() when inserting float/decimal values as they are stored as numerics in PostgreSQL and later converted to strings in Drizzle ORM
1015 2. **Query conditions**: Use and(...conditions) with spread operator, NOT and(conditions)
1016 3. **Joined results**: Access data via nested properties (result.table1.field, result.table2.field)
1017 4. **Test inputs**: Include ALL fields in test inputs, even those with Zod defaults
1018 5. **Type annotations**: Use SQL<unknown>[] for condition arrays
1019 6. **Query order**: Always apply .where() before .limit(), .offset(), or .orderBy()
1020 7. **Foreign key validation**: For INSERT/UPDATE operations with foreign keys, verify referenced entities exist first to prevent "violates foreign key constraint" errors. Ensure tests cover the use case where foreign keys are used.
1021 
1022 # Error Handling Best Practices:
1023 - Wrap database operations in try/catch blocks
1024 - Log the full error object with context: `console.error('Operation failed:', error);`
1025 - Rethrow original errors to preserve stack traces: `throw error;`
1026 - Error handling does not need to be tested in unit tests
1027 - Do not use other handlers in implementation or tests - keep fully isolated
1028 - NEVER use mocks - always test against real database operations
1029 
1030 
1031 Rules for changing files:
1032 - To apply local changes use SEARCH / REPLACE format.
1033 - To change the file completely use the WHOLE format.
1034 - When using SEARCH / REPLACE maintain precise indentation for both search and replace.
1035 - Each block starts with a complete file path followed by newline with content enclosed with pair of ```.
1036 - Each SEARCH / REPLACE block contains a single search and replace pair formatted with
1037 <<<<<<< SEARCH
1038 // code to find
1039 =======
1040 // code to replace it with
1041 >>>>>>> REPLACE
1042 
1043 
1044 Example WHOLE format:
1045 
1046 server/src/index.ts
1047 ```
1048 const t = initTRPC.create({{
1049   transformer: superjson,
1050 }});
1051 ```
1052 
1053 Example SEARCH / REPLACE format:
1054 
1055 server/src/helpers/reset.ts
1056 ```
1057 <<<<<<< SEARCH
1058 resetDB().then(() => console.log('DB reset successfully'));
1059 =======
1060 import {{ resetDB }} from '.';
1061 resetDB().then(() => console.log('DB reset successfully'));
1062 >>>>>>> REPLACE
1063 ```
1064 """.strip()
1065
results.issue_text: Possible SQL injection vector through string-based query construction.

162                 prompt = jinja2.Environment().from_string(prompt_template)

results.issue_text: By default, jinja2 sets autoescape to False. Consider using autoescape=True or use the select_autoescape function to mitigate XSS vulnerabilities.
